content:
In 100 words

Make a sample HCL object where I can group the following variables:

MAGLEV_CLIENT_EMAIL=my-service-account@my-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com
MAGLEV_INSTANCE_NAME=maglev-vm
MAGLEV_MACHINE_TYPE=e2-micro
MAGLEV_PRIVATE_KEY=.keys/private_key.pem
MAGLEV_PROJECT_ID=my-project
MAGLEV_ZONE=us-central1-a

Into another object which is a Kubernetes instance (not HA).

# Sample HCL Configuration

Here's an HCL object grouping your Maglev variables into a Kubernetes (non-HA) instance configuration:

```hcl
kubernetes_instance = {
  name    = "k8s-single-node"
  ha_mode = false

  maglev = {
    client_email = "my-service-account@my-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com"
    instance_name = "maglev-vm"
    machine_type = "e2-micro"
    private_key  = ".keys/private_key.pem"
    project_id   = "my-project"
    zone         = "us-central1-a"
  }
}
```

This nests the Maglev GCP credentials under a single Kubernetes instance object, keeping configuration clean and reusable.
